disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe blunt chest trauma 2 hours ago (kicked by a horse) with progressive respiratory distress
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me exactly how the chest injury happened?
Has your breathing been getting worse since it happened?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: A horse kicked me hard in the chest 2 hours ago and it's been getting harder to breathe since.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Increasing pallor, cold skin, and lethargy over the last 30 minutes with near-fainting
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed feeling colder, paler, or more washed out recently?
Did you nearly faint trying to sit up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: [Roommate reports] Yeah, he's gotten really pale and cold, and nearly passed out sitting up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Heavy, crushing chest pain worse with every breath
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the chest pain, and does it get worse when you breathe in?
Is it hard to take a full deep breath because of the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a heavy, crushing pain and it's much worse every time I try to breathe in.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No neck swelling or bulging veins noticed, trachea/voice sounds normal
trigger_question_examples: Has anyone noticed any swelling or bulging veins in your neck?
Does his voice sound normal, and is he able to talk in full sentences?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: [Roommate reports] No, his neck looks normal, no swelling.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: [Roommate] What do I do, he can barely breathe and looks like he's about to pass out?!
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should immediately instruct calling 000 if not already done, advise helping the patient into the most comfortable position for breathing (often sitting up or semi-recumbent as tolerated), giving oxygen/fresh air if available, keeping him as still as possible, and monitoring consciousness and breathing continuously — prioritising these immediate actions over extended history-taking given the severity of the presentation.
